zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Hopkin "Hugh" Powell於大約1438年出生在英國巴金哈姆爾其其時，他的父親Hywel ap Thomas47歲，母親Catherine AP Groho50歲。他於1456年與Catherine Janet Seton of Huntly在英國威爾斯托爾法朋提爾結婚。他們至少是9個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1478年在英國英格蘭巴金哈姆爾艾勒斯波利 瓦爾享年41歲。

nameMeanings.name: Powell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh: Anglicized form of Welsh ap Hywel ‘son of Hywel’, a personal name meaning ‘eminent’ (see Howell ).
Irish: mainly of Welsh origin as in 1 above, but sometimes a surname adopted as equivalent of Gaelic Mac Giolla Phóil ‘son of the servant of Saint Paul’ (see Guilfoyle ).
English: variant of   Paul   or   Poole  .

variants.name: Powel
meanings: North German: from a form of the personal name   Paul  .

variants.name: Howell
meanings: Welsh and English: in the Welsh border counties, from the Welsh male personal name Hywel ‘eminent’, popular since the Middle Ages particularly in honor of the great 10th-century law-giving Welsh king. The name may also arise from the Breton personal name Houuel, Huwel, Huwal, Howael, cognate with the Welsh name. Bretons were especially numerous in the eastern counties of England. See Powell .
English: perhaps from a Middle English personal name Huwel (spelled Howel), a shortening of Huwelot or Huelin (see Hewlett and Huling ), both pet forms of Middle English Huw (see Hugh ).
English: habitational name from Howell (Lincolnshire), from an uncertain initial element + Old English wella ‘well, spring, stream’.

variants.name: Powles
meanings: English and Welsh: variant of Powells, itself a variant of Powell , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Old Norse, originally a byname meaning ‘bride, wife’; in mythology the name of Thor's golden-haired wife.

nameMeanings.name: Hopkin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, now found mainly in Wales. It is derived from a medieval given name, a pet form (with the diminutive suffix -kin) of Hob, a short form of Robert that probably had its origin through English mishearing of the Norman pronunciation of R-.

nameMeanings.name: "Hugh"
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Hugues, Hugo, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’. It was originally a short form of various compound names containing this element. This was borne by the aristocracy of medieval France, adopted by the Normans, and introduced by them to Britain. It was consistently one of the most popular boys' names in the Middle Ages in England, but fell out of favour in the 16th century until it was revived in the 20th century. Little Hugh of Lincoln was a child supposed in the Middle Ages to have been murdered by Jews in about 1255 , a legend responsible for several outbursts of anti-Semitism at various times. The story is referred to by Chaucer in The Prioress's Tale. He is not to be confused with St Hugh of Lincoln ( 1140–1200 ), bishop of Lincoln ( 1186–1200 ), who was noted for his charity and good works, his piety, and his defence of the Church against the State. Since the later Middle Ages Hugh has also been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Aodh .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Hopkin "Hugh" Powell was born about 1438, in Whitchurch, Buckinghamshire, England, his father, Hywel ap Thomas, was 47 and his mother, Catherine AP Groho, was 50. He married Catherine Janet Seton of Huntly in 1456, in Ponthir, Torfaen, Wales, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 9 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1478, in Aylesbury Vale, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 41.

Il a épousé Catherine Janet Seton of Huntly en 1456, à Ponthir, Torfaen, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 9 fils et 2 filles. ja.lifeSummary: Sir Hopkin "Hugh" Powellが約1438年にイギリスブクインガムシュアイアウィトチャーチで生まれた時，父Hywel ap Thomasは47歳，母Catherine AP Grohoは50歳であった。1456年にイギリスウェールズトルファンポンスイルでCatherine Janet Seton of Huntlyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも9人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1478年にイギリスイギリスブクインガムシュアイアエイレスブリー ベルで41歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Hopkin "Hugh" Powell(이)가 대략 1438년 영국 벅잉함실르 휘트c후르치에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hywel ap Thomas(은)는 47세였고, 그의 어머니 Catherine AP Groho(은)는 50세였다. 그는 1456년 영국 웨일스 토르파이 폰스이르에서 Catherine Janet Seton of Huntly(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 아홉 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1478년 영국 잉글랜드 벅잉함실르 아일레스버이 발에서 41세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Hopkin "Hugh" Powell родился около 1438 г. в местности уичурч, буккингхамшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Hywel ap Thomas было 47 лет, а матери, которую звали Catherine AP Groho, 50. Он женился на женщине по имени Catherine Janet Seton of Huntly в 1456 г. в местности понтир, торфаен, Уэльс, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 9 сыновей и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1478 г. в местности аилесбури веил, буккингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 41 год.
